title: salix × pithoensis
intro: Salix × pithoensis, also known as the Pitho willow, is a hybrid of the weeping willow (Salix babylonica) and the white willow (Salix alba), which is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China. The bark of Salix × pithoensis contains salicylic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The medicinal properties of Salix × pithoensis include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for treating fever, headache, and rheumatism. Salix × pithoensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
